purpose: Configurable operational throughput caps for the deep-research-survey skill. The `scale` axis (SKILL.md, Modes and flags) selects a profile; `scale: <knobs>` overrides individual values per run. EDIT THE `wide` VALUES HERE to retune without touching SKILL.md or the phase files. This file is the single source of truth the skill reads at Phase-3 launch and Phase-5 verify.
status: The `wide` values are OPERATOR-SET PROVISIONAL (2026-06-20) — NOT yet calibrated. The calibration sweep (proposal section 6) validates-or-trims them; until it runs, wide mode relies on the safety-net invariants below to make any overrun recoverable. See the wide-mode calibration-sweep todo (upstream provenance).

searches_per_agent.note: wide=40 is ABOVE the only measured death boundary (~28 searches, the 2026-03 unbounded-query agent-death incident). Highest-risk knob; the calibration sweep must clear or trim it. Until then it leans entirely on the safety nets.

questions_per_agent.note: wide=7 was the 2026-03 'borderline' value (survivors carried 7). The breadth-vs-focus dimension stays bounded by the coverage gate (R-COVER / R-RUBRIC), not by this throughput knob.

verify_fanout.note: Phase-5 citation-verify concurrency. Real ceiling is `tool-unavailable` rate, not an agent-count sweet spot — verify agents are embarrassingly parallel (one source per agent).

downloads_per_day.note: SELF-IMPOSED budget only. The external Anna's-Archive service quota is the hard ceiling and is NOT documented in-repo — if the real limit is below this value, fetches beyond it fail regardless. A ceiling on intent, not a guarantee; a flag cannot lift the physical limit.

file_split_kb.note: Multi-file-architecture trigger. wide=200 KB accepts ~2x per-read token cost (~30-66K vs ~15-33K tokens/read).

safety_net_invariants: checkpoint-write to survey/_scratch/ after each sub-task (bounded loss on death)
event-driven death detection via the Workflow runtime, with the 15-minute poll as fallback
structured-output schemas on evidence/verify agents (a malformed return is caught)
synthesis stays on the main thread; agents collect raw evidence only
citation-integrity gate unchanged; downloads stay quota-bounded
bounded escalating retry on detected death (<=2 relaunches/agent, 2nd attempt trimmed, resume-from-checkpoint) runs BEFORE the main-thread fallback — see retry_policy

restart-intensity ceiling: if an agent class dies more than twice (i.e. retries exhausted), stop respawning, fall back to main-thread research, AND emit a visible coverage-gap marker into the report for that subtopic
invariant_rule: `scale: wide` is INVALID if any safety_net_invariant is disabled. Wide mode raises throughput; it never removes a safety net. The residual it does NOT close is 'alive but byzantine' (a confident-but-shallow return) — surfaced, not eliminated, by the coverage gate + a thin-return flag.

retry_policy.default: ON whenever evidence/verify agents are used (a safety net, not a toggleable quality flag); applies at ANY scale, most needed at wide.
escalation: attempt 1 (relaunch): SAME brief — rescues TRANSIENT deaths (flaky API/tool call, heavy-cluster context timeout).

attempt 2 (TRIMMED): rescues SYSTEMATIC deaths (brief above the death cliff). If at `wide`, drop to the `standard` search/question budget; if already at `standard`, split the cluster's questions or move the excess to the main thread.
retry_policy.resume_from_checkpoint: a relaunched agent reads its existing survey/_scratch/<agent>.md FIRST and continues from the last answered question — never redo answered questions (idempotent resume off the incremental checkpoint).
retry_policy.ceiling_composition: retry is BOUNDED BY the restart-intensity ceiling: after max_retries_per_agent (or >2 deaths in the class), STOP, fall back to main-thread research for that subtopic, and emit a visible coverage-gap marker. Retry NEVER overrides the ceiling (it is the circuit breaker that prevents a retry storm on a poison brief).
retry_policy.telemetry: record per-agent {deaths, retries, trimmed?, recovered-at-attempt} in the Phase-5 report footer; this is the death-rate field data the calibration sweep (items.json DRS-WIDE) consumes.
retry_policy.recovery_not_calibration: retry makes a `searches=40` overrun RECOVERABLE; it does NOT make searches=40 correct. The 2026-06 wide-mode evidence run measured ~29% silent death at searches=40 (2 of 4 dead clusters recovered only after the attempt-2 trim = systematic, not transient). Retry is recovery, NOT a substitute for the calibration sweep (the wide-mode calibration-sweep todo, upstream provenance); the telemetry above feeds that sweep.

evidence_agent_policy.default: ON — a default-on safety net (like retry_policy). It rewrites the broken schema-first / 5-question evidence-agent path; the legacy path is demoted to the `agent_hardening: off` fallback (gotcha-#9 broken-original case, not an additive enhancement).
evidence_agent_policy.rationale: Three 2026-06-25 evidence runs lost ~90% of clusters. Transcript-measured root cause: the per-agent ITERATION/STEP cap (~36-40 tool calls), NOT context size (a dead agent used ~58K of a 200K window) and NOT model tier (Sonnet survivors produced gold output). Agents spent the step budget on research — including 18 wasted Glob calls and full-page WebFetch — and were cut off mid-loop (final message stop_reason=tool_use, empty text) before the write step.

rules.deliverable_file_first: The incrementally-written survey/_scratch/<agent>.md file IS the graded deliverable; the agent writes it after EACH question (its chat return is a 2-line confirmation only). A structured-output schema is OPTIONAL; if one is used inside a Workflow parallel()/pipeline(), the agent() call MUST be try/catch-wrapped, because a missing StructuredOutput call THROWS (it does not return null) and a bare null-check retry is jumped over.
rules.no_glob_exact_paths: Give the agent EXACT file paths (local papers/model-cards/specs in download/ or docs/specs/, the exemplar file) in the brief. Forbid Glob / filesystem exploration — wasted Glob calls were the single largest step sink.
rules.webfetch_limited: Prefer WebSearch result snippets + targeted local-source grep/Read of line ranges; cap full-page WebFetch at <=2 per agent (each fetch is a step AND a context-bulk sink).
rules.empty_return_is_death: The orchestrator treats an empty-string agent return as a DEATH signal (if (!r || String(r).trim() === '')), not success — a step-capped agent COMPLETES with an empty final message, which a !!r check misreads.
rules.step_budget_headroom: Size each agent so every question keeps >= ~10 tool calls of headroom under the ~36-40-call step cap: with the waste removed, ~3-4 questions/agent (not the legacy 5). `knobs.questions_per_agent` is the UPPER bound; this is the effective working size. Splitting the same total questions across more agents raises per-question depth and breadth — it does NOT narrow or shallow the research (coverage is unchanged; synthesis still sees all files; R-COVER gates completeness).

evidence_agent_policy.off_restores: agent_hardening: off restores the legacy schema-first deliverable + 5-question sizing + unrestricted Glob/WebFetch (pre-2026-06-25 behavior). Provided for A/B and backward-compat; NOT recommended — it is the broken path.
evidence_agent_policy.status: SHIPPED default-ON 2026-06-25 (gotcha-#9 broken-original rewrite). Structural-checked. The death-RATE A/B is owed but deferred: it would re-run the exact expensive failure, and the mechanism is already transcript-measured. Refs (upstream provenance): the evidence-agent-hardening bug, the hardening decision, and the phase-3 file-first-deliverable todo.

report_footer_rule: Record the active scale (and any per-knob override) in the Phase-5 report footer next to mode/flags.
